articles.description: Explore the key provisions and implications of the U.S.-Iran agreement, including commitments, opposition from Israel, and unresolved issues.

articles.title: Shashi Tharoor's praise for PM Modi sparks political slugfest
articles.description: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor's assertion that Prime Minister Modi raised concerns about Indian civilian sailors with US President Trump has sparked internal party friction. This statement contradicts Rahul Gandhi's claim that the issue was ignored. The BJP has seized on Tharoor's remarks, highlighting the divergence within Congress and his praise for the Prime Minister's leadership.

articles.title: Donald Trump says no toll on Strait of Hormuz, threatens a US levy if Iran talks fail
articles.description: Donald Trump threatened that the US could impose its own toll on the passage through the Strait if the Iran talks don't yield results in the stipulated 60 days.

articles.title: Free pavements for walking, demand pedestrians
articles.description: Supreme Court calls footpath walking a fundamental right; Lucknow residents urge civic bodies to remove encroachments, illegal parking and repair damaged sidewalks.

articles.title: Vance rushes to Switzerland to pacify angry Iran as Netanyahu's strikes on Lebanon ruin peace deal?
articles.description: As U.S.-Iran negotiations move forward, questions are growing over who stands to gain the most from sanctions relief and economic recovery. Senior Iranian sources told Reuters that the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps could emerge as one of the biggest beneficiaries of a broader agreement. A future deal could unlock oil revenues, foreign investment and even a massive reconstruction fund estimated at $300 billion. Analysts note that the IRGC already maintains deep ties to sectors ranging from energy and shipping to telecommunications and construction. Critics warn that renewed business activity could further expand the Guards’ economic and political influence.

articles.title: Mojtaba trains guns at Israel as Bibi attacks Lebanon; Iran-USA deal officially off as Tehran fumes?
articles.description: Lebanon has become the breaking point for the fragile Iran–U.S. peace memorandum. Within 48 hours of the deal taking effect, Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon have reportedly killed more than 30 people, despite a U.S.-mediated ceasefire with Hezbollah announced by the IDF. Tehran is furious, threatening a strong response.

articles.title: Final nail in the coffin for Uddhav Thackeray's party? Amit Shah gives daring statement on Shiv Sena
articles.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah took a swipe at Uddhav Thackeray while addressing a public gathering in Kolhapur on June 20, as political turmoil deepened within Shiv Sena (UBT). Shah's remarks came after six of Shiv Sena (UBT)'s nine Lok Sabha MPs skipped a parliamentary meeting in Delhi and sought recognition as a separate group by writing to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. The rebel MPs are expected to formally join the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, a move that would further strengthen the BJP-led NDA government at the Centre.

articles.title: Bengal witnessing new freshness, moving to a brighter future: PM Modi
articles.description: PM Modi, in his first rally in West Bengal after BJP's historic win, said the state is seeing a 'new freshness'. At the 'Paschimbanga Divas' event, he stated that Bengal has broken its shackles and is moving towards a brighter future.

articles.title: Psychology says if you can't sleep until the trash is taken out, it may be your brain seeking closure
articles.description: People who can't sleep until the trash is taken out may be seeking mental closure, routine and a sense of control rather than simply keeping their homes clean.

articles.title: RS poll: Cong pins hope on CM for action against ‘betrayal’
articles.description: Jharkhand Rajya Sabha polls: Congress alleges cross-voting, urges CM Hemant Soren to probe “betrayal,” says all 16 MLAs backed Pranav Jha.

articles.title: Maharashtra To Centre: How 'Op Tiger' May Affect Eknath Shinde's Stature
articles.description: The rebellion by six Team Uddhav MPs will strengthen Shinde's position.

articles.title: NEET-UG: 3.56 lakh candidates to appear for re-exam across UP amid tight security
articles.description: To ensure peaceful and disruption-free conduct of the examination, large-scale police deployment has been made at the centres for security checks and law-and-order duties. The deployment includes 192 gazetted officers, 512 inspectors, 1,747 sub-inspectors, 2,152 head constables, 5,882 constables and 207 home guards. In addition, three companies of the Provincial Armed Constabulary (PAC) have been deployed in districts and one platoon of the PAC has been stationed in the Government Railway Police (GRP) jurisdiction.

articles.title: Pakistan says technical-level Iran-US talks on interim deal will begin Sunday in Switzerland
articles.description: Pakistan announced technical talks between Iran and the US on their interim deal will commence in Switzerland, with Qatar mediating. This comes as Iran, angered by Israeli attacks in Lebanon, declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again and expressed skepticism about the upcoming negotiations. The ongoing conflict in Lebanon, which has claimed numerous lives, threatens to derail the fragile US-Iran agreement.

articles.title: His admiration for PM appears to have transcended physical world: Congress slams Tharoor
articles.description: Congress has criticized Shashi Tharoor for praising Prime Minister Modi's handling of the Indian sailors' issue with President Trump. Party leader Pawan Khera accused Tharoor of hearing things Modi didn't say, citing a lack of official record on key discussions. Tharoor defended his remarks, emphasizing the importance of protecting civilian sailors and accusing critics of prioritizing politics over citizen safety.

articles.title: Iran warns US deal 'in trouble' if not implemented soon, sends delegation to Switzerland
articles.description: Iran warned that its agreement with the United States could face difficulties unless key commitments are implemented, while confirming that negotiators will travel to Switzerland for follow-up talks.

articles.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire after continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon
articles.description: Iran has closed the strait of Hormuz – citing alleged ⁠violations of a ceasefire agreement ​by the US ‌and Israel. Tehran said the closure was the "first step" in response ​to what it described ​as breaches ​of commitments regarding the continuing clashes in Lebanon - and warned ​that further measures would be taken if "aggression" continued. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani, and FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.

articles.title: Re-NEET 2026: Bengaluru Declares 200-Metre Restricted Zone Around Centres, Shops Shut
articles.description: To facilitate the smooth conduct of Re-NEET 2026, Bengaluru's police and district administration have established a 200-meter prohibited zone around examination centres.

articles.title: Karnataka BJP turmoil: Rajegowda demands R. Ashok's resignation
articles.description: BJP leader D Rajegowda demands the removal of Karnataka BJP president R. Ashok, citing failure to control MLA cross-voting. He urged the BJP central leadership to intervene, warning of a looming crisis in the state party unit.

articles.title: DK Shivakumar meets NRK delegation to strengthen Karnataka's global ties
articles.description: Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar met a high-level delegation of Non-Resident Kannadigas from 30 countries to strengthen ties. He also presided over a review meeting to assess the progress of the state's flagship 'Pancha Guarantee' schemes.

articles.title: ‘CM Vijay’s photo on wall, reels in classroom’: Annamalai targets TVK
articles.description: Annamalai has urged CM Vijay and the Minister for School Education to avoid recurrence of such incidents.

articles.title: Trump-Iran ceasefire sparks market rally but uncertainty remains over lasting peace: Report
articles.description: Despite the optimism surrounding the ceasefire, Jefferies remains sceptical that the agreement will hold. The report argues that any arrangement perceived as overly favourable to Iran could face resistance from Washington's security establishment and alter long-standing geopolitical alliances in the region.

articles.title: Union Home Secretary reviews Jammu and Kashmir security grid ahead of Amarnath Yatra
articles.description: Jammu Kashmir: Home Secretary reviews Amarnath Yatra security plans in Srinagar as officials assess routes, deployment, intelligence sharing, camps, rescue systems and coordination among agencies before the annual pilgrimage begins amid heightened security measures across the Union Territory.

articles.title: ‘Only one Shiv Sena remains’: Amit Shah's dig at Uddhav Thackeray, says Eknath Shinde's no longer a ‘faction’
articles.description: Union hone minister and BJP leader's declaration caps a split years in the making, even as allegations of Centre's ruling party “buying” MPs fly

articles.title: ‘Pakistan doesn't have press freedom’: JD Vance blames Islamabad for delay in releasing Iran deal details
articles.description: JD Vance cited Pakistan's lack of US-style press freedom while explaining the delay in releasing the full text of the US-Iran peace agreement.

articles.title: 'Insult to Hindu faith': BJP slams Congress for depicting Rahul Gandhi as Lord Parshuram; row erupts
articles.description: A political storm has erupted in Varanasi after Congress workers depicted Rahul Gandhi as Lord Parshuram, performing a symbolic milk ablution on his photograph. The BJP has strongly condemned the act, accusing the Congress of disrespecting Hindu faith and using religious symbols for political gain. Congress leaders, however, defended the gesture, questioning the BJP's monopoly on religious figures and asserting Gandhi's distinct identity.

articles.title: PM Modi wishes President Murmu on birthday, joins her in Odisha to launch Rs 47,600 crore projects
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi celebrated President Droupadi Murmu's birthday by highlighting her dedication to public service before joining her in Odisha. Their visit included engagements in her native Mayurbhanj district and the inauguration of development projects exceeding Rs 47,600 crore. These initiatives span energy, infrastructure, healthcare, and more, aiming to boost connectivity and create jobs across the state.

articles.title: A $300-billion opportunity? Why Iran's Revolutionary Guards are watching US talks closely
articles.description: As Washington and Tehran pursue a deal, Iran's Revolutionary Guards could emerge stronger. Oil export waivers, reconstruction funds and business ties may expand IRGC influence, raising concerns for investors and Western governments.

articles.title: "5-Year Consensual Relation Can't Be Termed Rape": Allahabad Court's Big Order
articles.description: Court ruled that a long-term consensual relationship between two adults cannot be treated as rape merely because a promise to marry was not fulfilled

articles.title: 'Kuch log...': Sanjay Raut counters Eknath Shinde's 'sher akela aata hai' remark with loyal dog post
articles.description: Political tensions escalate between Shiv Sena factions as Sanjay Raut fires back at Eknath Shinde's 'lion' jibe with a post calling some people disloyal dogs. This follows speculation of six Uddhav Thackeray camp MPs potentially defecting to Shinde's side. Raut announced action, including disqualification efforts, against the absent MPs, while Thackeray emotionally appealed to workers, vowing not to hand the party to 'thieves'.

articles.title: Bihar court grants interim relief to educator Khan Sir in coaching institute firing case
articles.description: A Bihar court granted interim relief to popular educator Faisal Khan, known as 'Khan Sir', in a firing and vandalism case. The court issued a 'no coercive action' order until the next hearing. Police had registered an FIR against Khan Sir and two others following an incident at his coaching institute where two guards were arrested based on video evidence of aerial firing and vandalism.

articles.title: ‘Some Are Dogs, But Not Loyal’: Sanjay Raut’s Veiled Message Amid 'Operation Tiger' Buzz In Shiv Sena UBT
articles.description:
A provocative social media post by Shiv Sena (UBT) leader and Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Raut has further escalated tensions within the party, which is already grappling with reports of a possible rebellion by several of its Members of Parliament. Though Raut did not name anyone directly, the timing of the post has sparked widespread speculation that it was aimed at party leaders accused of plotting to abandon the Uddhav Thackeray-led faction.

Social Media Post Adds To Political Storm

The cryptic message comes as Shiv Sena (UBT) faces one of its most serious internal challenges since the dramatic 2022 split that saw Eknath Shinde break away with a large section of legislators and eventually form a new government in Maharashtra.

Raut’s post, shared on Saturday, featured an image bearing the Hindi message, “Kuch log kuttey toh hoty hain, lekin wafa’daar nahi hoty” (Some people may be dogs, but they are not loyal). He accompanied the image with a brief caption: “Jai Maharashtra!”

जय महाराष्ट्र ! pic.twitter.com/nYaiVHLv7M

— Sanjay Raut (@rautsanjay61) June 20, 2026


ALSO READ: 'If You Don't Trust Me, I'll Step Down': Uddhav To Party Workers Amid Shiv Sena Split Crisis

Recent developments have triggered fears of another setback for the Uddhav camp, with reports suggesting that a group of Lok Sabha MPs may be preparing to chart an independent course.

War Of Words Between Rival Sena Camps Intensifies

The latest controversy unfolded against the backdrop of sharp exchanges between leaders of the rival Shiv Sena factions.

Addressing party workers at a recent rally, Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde brushed aside criticism from opponents and projected confidence in his leadership.

He said, “Some people have been barking for the last few days. They bark in groups, but a tiger walks alone. This is Shiv Sena.”

Six MPs Missing From Meeting Trigger Defection Speculation

The internal crisis gained momentum earlier this week when six of Shiv Sena (UBT)’s nine Lok Sabha MPs failed to attend a parliamentary party meeting in New Delhi despite a party whip being issued.

Only Arvind Sawant, Anil Desai and Rajabhau Waje attended the gathering and publicly reaffirmed their support for Uddhav Thackeray, as per reports.

The absent MPs included Nagesh Aashtikar, Sanjay Deshmukh, Sanjay Jadhav, Sanjay Dina Patil, Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Bhausaheb Wakchaure. Their absence fueled speculation that a coordinated move may be underway.

Responding to the development, Raut launched a blistering attack on the dissidents. He also confirmed that disciplinary proceedings had been initiated against the lawmakers.

ALSO READ: This Is Just The Trailer’: Eknath Shinde Signals Bigger 2.0 Political Turbulence For Uddhav Camp

Uddhav Camp Vows To Protect Balasaheb’s Legacy

Adding to the intrigue, Shiv Sena MLC Chandrakant Raghuvanshi recently claimed that six MPs had already expressed confidence in Shinde’s leadership, further fueling talk of an operation dubbed “Operation Tiger.”

Despite the mounting uncertainty, Raut maintained that the party remains committed to the ideals of its founder, Balasaheb Thackeray.

He said that Shiv Sena has completed 60 years and Balasaheb Thackeray’s birth centenary was going on. They always draw inspiration from Balasaheb Thackeray and they would continue to fight until the evil is eradicated.

articles.description: The petitioners questioned whether teachers who joined the exercise did so voluntarily, while the EC maintained they had joined of their own accord and were performing a “national duty”.

articles.title: TMC, BJP Workers Clash Outside Kolkata Airport During Abhishek Banerjee's Return From Delhi
articles.description: Supporters of the Trinamool Congress and the BJP clashed outside Kolkata Airport as Abhishek Banerjee returned from Delhi. Police had to intervene after the confrontation turned violent, causing panic among passengers and major traffic disruptions.Abhishek Banerjee was escorted out under heavy security while the clash unfolded outside the arrival terminal.

articles.title: Yoga can unite society, nation, and world, says Baba Ramdev ahead of IYD
articles.description: Baba Ramdev described yoga as a unifying force for health and harmony, calling it a way of life. He lauded PM Modi and AP CM Chandrababu Naidu as 'karma yogis' ahead of International Yoga Day, which is being celebrated across the country.

articles.title: Sena not a plot of land one can put a name on: Shinde
articles.description: Contrary to speculations, the six breakaway Sena (UBT) MPs who have reportedly submitted a letter to the Lok Sabha speaker seeking a merger with the Shinde-led Sena did not turn up at the foundation day programme organised by the Sena

articles.title: Iran is the biggest winner from US truce push: Jefferies report
articles.description: Iran has emerged as the biggest beneficiary of the proposed US-Iran ceasefire framework, according to Jefferies, which says the deal could unlock billions of dollars in frozen funds and ease sanctions on Tehran. The brokerage also flagged rising AI exuberance, sticky inflation and growing market concentration as key themes shaping the global investment landscape.

articles.description: The government also restored the general consent for prosecution -- revoked by the then TMC regime in 2018 -- last week.

articles.description: Bidadi township project: JD(S) leaders including MLC TA Sharavana have petitioned the Karnataka government to scrap the proposed township citing farmers’ opposition to land acquisition and urging officials to reconsider the development plan in Bengaluru rural region
